---
title: "Projects"
---
## Featured Projects

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

### QIntel Application – Live Event Q&A System with AI Extension
**R + Shiny • SQLite • AI Embeddings (In Progress)**

Built a real-time audience question management system for large events. AI module using text embeddings for duplicate detection and topic classification.

[Live Demo](https://qevent.shinyapps.io/qevent_app/) | [Final Report](Final Project.pdf) | [Presentation](Presentation.pdf)
:::


### Live Application

You can interact with the live system below.

<iframe src="https://qevent.shinyapps.io/qevent_app/" width="100%" height="900px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

**Read the full report:** [Final Project.pdf](Final%20Project.pdf) **View the presentation:** [Presentation.pdf](Presentation.pdf)

## AI Extension (In Progress)

I am developing an AI module that:

-   Detects duplicate questions using text embeddings\
-   Classifies questions by topic\
-   Suggests answers based on historical responses\
-   Prevents repetitive submissions\
-   Displays questions and answers live on screen

### Why This Matters

Large meetings often suffer from repeated questions and missed answers. This system reduces redundancy, improves clarity, and enhances the experience for both speakers and participants.

### Technologies

-   **Frontend:** R + Shiny\
-   **Database:** SQLite / PostgreSQL\
-   **AI Engine:** Embedding models for semantic similarity\
-   **Documentation:** Quarto

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Flight Delay Prediction (DFW)

A predictive modeling project analyzing weather patterns, operational factors, and propagated delays to identify airport pairs that should not share a pilot due to weather-related delays.I used Lasso, Random Forest for methodologies for this project.

**Tech:** R, tidyverse, glmnet, randomForest

[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/susanmohammadk22/airline-weather-risk-analysis)

## NBA Player Stats & Gallery

This application provides a dynamic interface to explore NBA player data stored in a relational database. By integrating a SQLite backend with a Shiny front-end, I created a tool that allows for real-time data filtering and image rendering.

<iframe src="https://qevent.shinyapps.io/nba-final/" width="100%" height="600px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

[View Source Code on GitHub](https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/NBA-Project)

**Technical Stack:** R (Shiny), SQLite, DT (DataTables), CSS.
